name: Cranberry-Fig Chutney
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Cranberries, coarsely chopped (12 oz. package)
1 One-inch knob ginger root, peeled and finely shredded
1 Large Navel orange, quartered and finely chopped
5 Dried figs, finely snipped (Calamynia or Black Mission)
1/2 cup Walnuts, toasted and coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine cranberries, orange, onion, currants, figs, walnuts, mustard seed, shredded ginger, vinegar, and whiskey in a 4-quart saucepan.
2. In a small bowl, mix together brown sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, salt, and cayenne pepper.
3. Add the dry ingredients to the saucepan and stir to combine.
4. Heat the mixture to boiling, then reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes, stirring frequently.
5. Allow the chutney to cool before refrigerating it for up to 2 weeks. It can be frozen for up to 1 year.
NOTE: If not using whiskey, use a total of 2 large Navel oranges, chopped, instead.
